page_content:
KNES305 Principles & Application of Exercise Rehabilitation (3 Credits) In-depth analysis of the basic principles of rehabilitation and exercise programming for common injuries encountered in a clinical setting. Basic assessment techniques, ranges of motion, muscular anatomy, rehabilitative protocols, and exercise modalities are a major focus. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in BSCI201 and BSCI202; and 1 course with a minimum grade of C- from (KNES300, KNES350, KNES360, KNES370, KNES385). Restriction: Must be in a major within the SPHL-Kinesiology department; and must have earned a minimum of 75 credits. Credit Only Granted for: KNES498T or KNES305. Formerly: KNES498T.

KNES306 Prosthetics for Limb Amputations (3 Credits) Introduction to the science of prosthetics and the artiﬁcial devices that are designed, developed and ﬁt to replace the missing body part or parts lost through trauma, disease or congenital conditions. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in BSCI201, BSCI202, and KNES300. Restriction: Must be in a major within the SPHL-Kinesiology department; and must have earned a minimum of 75 credits. Credit Only Granted for: KNES306 or KNES498W. Formerly: KNES498W.
